# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.
# Category: api_gateway_security

_format_version: "3.0"

services:
  - name: users-service
    host: users-api.internal.example.com
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 5
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - microservice
      - users
      - v1
    plugins:
      # JWT authentication for user services
      - name: jwt
        enabled: true
        config:
          uri_param_names: ["jwt"] # Allows JWT to be passed as a query parameter
      # ACL for role-based access to user services
      - name: acl
        enabled: true
        config:
          whitelist_groups:
            - admin
            - registered-user
      # Add custom headers to upstream requests
      - name: request-transformer
        config:
          add:
            headers:
              - X-Service-Name: users-service
              - X-API-Version: v1
        enabled: true

  - name: products-service
    host: products-api.internal.example.com
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 5
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - microservice
      - products
      - v1
    plugins:
      # Rate limiting for public-facing product API
      - name: rate-limiting
        enabled: true
        config:
          minute: 100
          hour: 1000
          policy: local
          fault_tolerant: true
          hide_client_headers: false
      # Remove sensitive/unwanted headers from upstream responses
      - name: response-transformer
        enabled: true
        config:
          remove:
            headers:
              - X-Powered-By
              - Server
              - X-Response-Time
      # Basic caching for product listings
      - name: proxy-cache
        enabled: true
        config:
          content_type:
            - application/json
          cache_key_header:
            - Accept-Encoding
          cache_ttl: 60 # Cache for 60 seconds

  - name: orders-service
    host: orders-api.internal.example.com
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 5
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - microservice
      - orders
      - v1
    plugins:
      # JWT authentication required for orders
      - name: jwt
        enabled: true
      # ACL for internal groups accessing order services
      - name: acl
        enabled: true
        config:
          whitelist_groups:
            - admin
            - order-manager
            - registered-user
      # Health check for the service
      - name: health-check
        enabled: true
        config:
          path: /healthz
          interval: 5
          timeout: 3
          unhealthy_statuses: [404, 500, 502, 503, 504]
          healthy_statuses: [200, 201, 202, 203, 204, 205, 206, 300, 301, 302, 303, 304, 307, 308]

routes:
  - name: users-api-route
    paths:
      - /api/v1/users
      - /api/users
    strip_path: true # Remove the path prefix before forwarding
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - PATCH
      - DELETE
    service: users-service
    tags:
      - external-api
      - users

  - name: products-api-route
    paths:
      - /api/v1/products
      - /api/products
    strip_path: true
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - PATCH
      - DELETE
    service: products-service
    tags:
      - external-api
      - products

  - name: orders-api-route
    paths:
      - /api/v1/orders
      - /api/orders
    strip_path: true
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - PATCH
      - DELETE
    service: orders-service
    tags:
      - external-api
      - orders

plugins:
  # Global Prometheus metrics exporter
  - name: prometheus
    global: true
    enabled: true
    config:
      per_consumer: true # Export metrics per consumer
  # Global correlation ID for tracing requests across services
  - name: correlation-id
    global: true
    enabled: true
    config:
      header_name: X-Request-ID
      generator: uuid
      echo_upstream_header: true
  # Global key-auth for internal/admin APIs (if applicable)
  - name: key-auth
    global: false # Apply globally if needed, or specifically to routes/services
    enabled: true
    config:
      key_names: ["apikey"]
  # Global IP restriction for gateway access (e.g., from specific CIDRs)
  - name: ip-restriction
    global: true
    enabled: true
    config:
      whitelist:
        - 192.168.1.0/24 # Example internal network
        - 10.0.0.0/8    # Example internal network
        - 203.0.113.0/24 # Example trusted external network
      # blacklist: # Example: if you need to block specific IPs
      #   - 198.51.100.1
  # Global logging plugin for access logs
  - name: http-log
    global: true
    enabled: true
    config:
      http_endpoint: http://log-aggregator.internal.example.com/kong-logs
      method: POST
      headers:
        Content-Type: application/json
      custom_fields_by_lua:
        - "kong.client.ip"
        - "kong.request.get_header('User-Agent')"
        - "kong.route.name"
        - "kong.service.name"
        - "kong.consumer.username"
        - "ngx.var.status"
        - "ngx.var.request_time"
        - "ngx.var.bytes_sent"
        - "ngx.var.request_length"
        - "ngx.var.http_x_request_id" # Assuming correlation-id plugin uses this
      queue:
        max_batch_size: 100
        max_bytes: 1048576 # 1MB
        flush_timeout: 5 # Flush every 5 seconds
        initial_retry_delay: 0.1
        max_retry_delay: 5
        retries: 5
  # OIDC plugin for external authentication (e.g., Okta, Auth0)
  # Uncomment and configure if needed, often applied per service/route
  # - name: openid-connect
  #   global: false
  #   enabled: true
  #   config:
  #     issuer: https://dev-xxxxxx.okta.com/oauth2/default
  #     client_id: {your_client_id}
  #     client_secret: {your_client_secret}
  #     redirect_uri: https://your-gateway.example.com/callback
  #     session_secret: {random_string_for_session_encryption}
  #     discovery_timeout: 5000
  #     introspect_uri: https://dev-xxxxxx.okta.com/oauth2/default/v1/introspect
  #     introspection_timeout: 5000
  #     ssl_verify_upstream: true
  #     realm: "Kong OIDC Realm"
  #     logout_paths:
  #       - /logout
  #     bearer_only: false
  #     auth_methods:
  #       - password
  #       - client_credentials
  #       - authorization_code
